# Split in the Polaris App

> How to split pETH into fpETH and vpETH, merge them back, and avoid common execution mistakes.

Canonical URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split
Markdown URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/split.md
Section: Using the App
Updated: 2026-06-09
Last verified: 2026-06-09

Documentation index: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/llms.txt
Full documentation bundle: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/llms-full.txt

---

## What it does

Split converts pETH into matching fpETH and vpETH at a fixed **1:1:1** ratio — no fees, no oracle, and fully reversible: merge 1 fpETH + 1 vpETH back into 1 pETH at any time. **fpETH** is the floor leg, redeemable for the bonding curve's floor price (which only ever rises), so it gains in ETH terms with little volatility. **vpETH** is the above-floor leg, absorbing all of pETH's volatility above the floor. Use this page for the workflow; use [pETH Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split) for the mechanism.

Image: Polaris Split tab with a Split/Merge toggle, fpETH and vpETH outputs, live floor and market prices, and a price simulator

*Split and Merge change your pETH exposure; the "What if…" simulator shows how each leg responds to price moves.*

## Before you start

- Start from the [Dashboard](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app) and verify the current app and network first.
- Hold pETH for splitting, or equal fpETH and vpETH amounts for merging.
- Confirm which leg you intend to hold after the action — they behave very differently.

## Steps/checks

1. Choose Split or Merge.
2. Enter the amount and review the expected fpETH + vpETH (or pETH) output.
3. Approve pETH, fpETH, or vpETH if required.
4. Sign the transaction.
5. Confirm wallet balances update.

A split worked when pETH decreases and fpETH plus vpETH each increase by the same amount. A merge worked when equal fpETH and vpETH decrease and pETH increases.

## Common failures

- Merge amount exceeds the smaller of your fpETH or vpETH balances.
- Token balance exists but is hidden in the wallet UI.
- Approval was granted for one leg but not the other.
- You expected holding one leg to behave like holding unsplit pETH.

## Risks/links

Holding only fpETH or only vpETH changes your exposure: fpETH tracks the floor with little volatility, while vpETH amplifies upside and downside. Read [pETH Split](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/split), [pETH Floor Price](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/peth/floor-price), and [Risk Disclosure](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure) before relying on either leg.

## Next actions

- Use [Borrow](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/borrow) (the ETH sub-tab) to borrow ETH against fpETH.
- Use [Earn](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/using-app/earn) to stake vpETH for a pETH yield stream.
- Merge in this tab to recombine the legs into pETH.
- Use [Troubleshooting](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/troubleshooting) if a split or merge reverts.

Relevant app/search vocabulary: Swap, swap, Split, fpETH, vpETH, pETH, floor price.
